name: dam_combat_guard6_agealign
description: dam_combat_guard6 with the native input timeline delayed by 382 frames (1146 sim ticks) so both sides' guards have the SAME AGE at motion onset (FID-0054 guard-age alignment). The stock recipe spends stock_gameplay_start_global=1146 sim ticks in briefing/intro between mission start (g_GlobalTimer=0, all 36 guards spawned byte-identical to native) and gameplay input frame 0; the native direct --level boot spends ~0. That 1145-tick guard-age skew is a capture-RECIPE artifact that inflates every patrolling guard's cross-side position delta under compare_combat_trace --align tick (native onset global 241 vs stock 1386). This route shifts every native event by +382 frames (80->462, 100->482) so native onset lands at global 3*462+1=1387, within 1 tick of stock's 1386 (3-tick frame granularity floor). Residual guard divergence under this route is the SIM-layer component only (WAYMODE_MAGIC patrol semantics, FID-0014/FID-0012 cluster) plus RNG-value differences, with the recipe layer removed. Analysis: movement_oracle_capture.sh --route dam_combat_guard6_agealign --native-full-trace --no-compare, then compare_combat_trace.py --align tick.
